title: Diasperus arnottianus
taxonomy: Diasperus arnottianus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. This family consists of a diverse group of plants commonly known as the spurge family. The classification of Diasperus arnottianus is further broken down into the genus Diasperus and the species arnottianus. The species is native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in India. It is a rare and threatened species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.

intro: Diasperus arnottianus, also known as the Chinese or Indian earthnut, is a type of tuberous plant native to Southeast Asia and India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, inflammation, and improve overall well-being.
